source: Willcox, G. (1982), The Feeling Wheel. Secondary words follow the Willcox/Roberts lineage; tertiary words curated for ND Toolbox.

nodes.label: Joyful
nodes.definition: A bright, warm feeling of gladness or delight.

guidance.pointsTo: Joy often shows up when something matters to you and is going well.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might pause a moment to let the good feeling land.

nodes.label: Content
nodes.definition: Quietly satisfied; you have enough right now.

guidance.pointsTo: Contentment often points to a need that is, for now, met.
guidance.oneOption: You might simply notice what is going right, no need to change anything.

nodes.label: Satisfied
nodes.definition: Pleased with how something turned out.

nodes.label: Fulfilled
nodes.definition: Full and complete, as if something meaningful is done.

guidance.pointsTo: Happiness often follows a moment that fits what you value.
guidance.oneOption: You might name out loud one thing that sparked it.

nodes.label: Delighted
nodes.definition: Sharply, happily surprised by something good.

nodes.label: Glad
nodes.definition: Simply pleased that something is so.

nodes.label: Excited
nodes.definition: Lit up and energized about what is ahead.

guidance.pointsTo: Excitement often points to something you are looking forward to.
guidance.oneOption: You might channel a little of the energy into one small first step.

guidance.pointsTo: Playfulness often shows up when you feel safe enough to be loose.
guidance.oneOption: You might give yourself permission to follow it for a few minutes.

nodes.label: Amused
nodes.definition: Quietly entertained; something struck you as funny.

nodes.label: Lighthearted
nodes.definition: Free of weight or worry for now.

guidance.pointsTo: Optimism often points to a sense that effort here is worth it.
guidance.oneOption: You might jot one hope so you can return to it later.

nodes.label: Inspired
nodes.definition: Moved to make or do something.

nodes.label: Interested
nodes.definition: Drawn in; wanting to know more.

guidance.pointsTo: Interest often points to something worth your attention.
guidance.oneOption: You might follow the curiosity one question further.

nodes.label: Powerful
nodes.definition: A feeling of capability, confidence, and agency.

guidance.pointsTo: A sense of power often points to something you can affect or do.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might name the one thing that is yours to act on.

nodes.label: Confident
nodes.definition: Sure of yourself and what you can do.

guidance.pointsTo: Confidence often points to skill or preparation you can trust.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one recent success stand as evidence.

nodes.label: Empowered
nodes.definition: Free and able to act on your own terms.

nodes.label: Proud
nodes.definition: Warmly pleased with something you did or are.

guidance.pointsTo: Pride often points to effort or values you lived up to.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself take quiet credit for it.

nodes.label: Accomplished
nodes.definition: You achieved something that took work.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling respected often points to a need to be taken seriously being met.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice who offered it, and let it register.

nodes.label: Admired
nodes.definition: Looked up to for who you are or what you do.

nodes.label: Capable
nodes.definition: Up to the task in front of you.

guidance.pointsTo: Capability often points to a match between the task and your skills.
guidance.oneOption: You might start with the part you already know how to do.

nodes.label: Resourceful
nodes.definition: Good at finding a way through.

guidance.pointsTo: Determination often points to a goal that matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might define the next single step and stop there.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling appreciated often points to a need to be seen being met.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the acknowledgment fully arrive before moving on.

nodes.label: Important
nodes.definition: Your presence makes a difference here.

guidance.pointsTo: Peace often shows up when nothing urgent is pulling at you.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might slow one breath and stay here a beat longer.

guidance.pointsTo: Calm often points to a nervous system that feels safe enough.
guidance.oneOption: You might let your shoulders drop and notice the quiet.

guidance.pointsTo: Relaxation often points to a load that has, for now, lifted.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one part of your body soften a little more.

guidance.pointsTo: Security often points to a need for safety being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice one thing that is holding you steady.

nodes.label: Grounded
nodes.definition: Rooted and present in your body.

nodes.label: Protected
nodes.definition: Shielded from what could harm you.

guidance.pointsTo: Love often points to a connection that matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the person know, in a small way, if it feels right.

nodes.label: Grateful
nodes.definition: Thankful for something you have or received.

guidance.pointsTo: Gratitude often points to something good you have noticed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name one thing, however small, that you are glad of.

nodes.label: Warm
nodes.definition: Softly glowing toward someone or something.

nodes.label: Trusting
nodes.definition: Willing to be open and rely on someone.

guidance.pointsTo: Trust often points to a bond that has felt safe over time.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one small honesty pass between you.

nodes.label: Sad
nodes.definition: A heavy, low feeling, often tied to loss.

guidance.pointsTo: Sadness often points to something you care about that has been lost or missed.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might let the feeling be here without needing to fix it.

guidance.pointsTo: Loneliness often points to a need for connection that is not being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might send one small message to someone you trust — no pressure to.

nodes.label: Abandoned
nodes.definition: Left behind by someone you counted on.

nodes.label: Disconnected
nodes.definition: Present with others but not in contact.

guidance.pointsTo: Hurt often points to something that mattered being damaged or dismissed.
guidance.oneOption: You might place a hand where you feel it and breathe once, slowly.

nodes.label: Wounded
nodes.definition: Cut deeply by words or actions.

nodes.label: Heartbroken
nodes.definition: In deep grief over a loss.

nodes.label: Disappointed
nodes.definition: Let down when things fell short.

guidance.pointsTo: Disappointment often points to a hope that did not come to pass.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you had wanted, plainly, to yourself.

nodes.label: Depressed
nodes.definition: Heavily low, often flat or empty.

guidance.pointsTo: This heaviness often points to being depleted or grieving, not to a flaw in you.
guidance.oneOption: You might make the next step very small — a glass of water counts.

nodes.label: Empty
nodes.definition: Hollow, as if something is missing.

nodes.label: Numb
nodes.definition: Feeling little or nothing at all.

nodes.label: Guilty
nodes.definition: Uneasy that you did something wrong.

guidance.pointsTo: Guilt often points to a value of yours that you feel you crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might ask what, if anything, is actually yours to repair.

nodes.label: Ashamed
nodes.definition: Wanting to hide, as if you are bad.

nodes.label: Remorseful
nodes.definition: Sorry and wishing you had acted differently.

nodes.label: Vulnerable
nodes.definition: Exposed and open to being hurt.

guidance.pointsTo: Vulnerability often points to something tender being close to the surface.
guidance.oneOption: You might choose who, if anyone, is safe enough to see it.

nodes.label: Mad
nodes.definition: A charged feeling that something is wrong or unfair.

guidance.pointsTo: Anger often points to a boundary crossed or a need going unmet.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might ask what the anger is trying to protect.

guidance.pointsTo: Frustration often points to an obstacle between you and a goal.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the specific blocker; it is often smaller once named.

nodes.label: Fed up
nodes.definition: Done with putting up with it.

guidance.pointsTo: Irritation often points to a small, repeated friction — or to being depleted.
guidance.oneOption: You might check whether you are hungry, tired, or overstimulated first.

guidance.pointsTo: Resentment often points to a hurt that was never fully addressed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the unmet expectation underneath it.

nodes.label: Hostile
nodes.definition: Strongly angry, ready to push back.

guidance.pointsTo: Hostility often points to feeling attacked or deeply wronged.
guidance.oneOption: You might give the heat somewhere safe to go — move, or step away.

nodes.label: Seething
nodes.definition: Anger held tightly under the surface.

nodes.label: Jealous
nodes.definition: Threatened by what someone else has.

guidance.pointsTo: Jealousy often points to something you want or fear losing.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you actually want underneath the comparison.

nodes.label: Possessive
nodes.definition: Afraid of losing someone or something.

nodes.label: Disrespected
nodes.definition: Treated as if you do not matter.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling disrespected often points to a need to be valued being crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might decide what boundary, if any, you want to name later.

nodes.label: Scared
nodes.definition: A guarded feeling of threat or uncertainty.

guidance.pointsTo: Fear often points to something that feels unsafe or unknown.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might ask whether the danger is here now, or anticipated.

nodes.label: Anxious
nodes.definition: Keyed up and uneasy about what might happen.

guidance.pointsTo: Anxiety often points to uncertainty your mind is trying to solve ahead of time.
guidance.oneOption: You might bring attention to one thing you can sense right now.

nodes.label: Worried
nodes.definition: Turning a concern over and over.

nodes.label: Uneasy
nodes.definition: Something feels off, hard to name.

nodes.label: Insecure
nodes.definition: Unsure of your footing or worth.

guidance.pointsTo: Insecurity often points to a fear of not being enough in this moment.
guidance.oneOption: You might recall one time you handled something like this.

nodes.label: Self-conscious
nodes.definition: Overly aware of being watched or judged.

guidance.pointsTo: Overwhelm often points to more input or demand than capacity right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might reduce the field to the single next action, and hide the rest.

nodes.label: Swamped
nodes.definition: Buried under too much to do.

guidance.pointsTo: Rejection often points to a need to belong that feels threatened.
guidance.oneOption: You might remind yourself that one no is not a verdict on your worth.

nodes.label: Unwanted
nodes.definition: As if your presence is not welcome.

nodes.label: Confused
nodes.definition: Unable to make sense of things.

guidance.pointsTo: Confusion often points to missing information or mixed signals.
guidance.oneOption: You might write down the one question you most want answered.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling threatened often points to a real or perceived danger to you or what you hold.
guidance.oneOption: You might create a little distance or a barrier while you assess.
